div1.entryFree: Jet, vb. to stalk, to strut: how he --s under his advanced plumes, Tw. II, 5, 36. arched so high that giants may j. through, Cymb. III, 3, 5. whose men and dames so --ed and adorned, Per. I, 4, 26. With on, == to insult over, to treat with insolence: tyranny begins to j. upon the aweless throne, R3 II, 4, 51*(Ff jut). to j. upon a prince's right, Tit. II, 1, 64 (Qq iet, Ff set). In Err. II, 2, 28 O. Edd. jest.
